checks.a_analysis: I have compared the output JSON against the source PDF page by page. Every paragraph and instruction in the output corresponds directly to the text found in the original document. No fabricated body text or invented instructions were found. All image alt text is within acceptable AI-generated parameters.

The source document contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or mailing addresses. It refers to network names (SSIDs) like 'NCGov-Guest'. I verified these names in the output and they match the source exactly. No URLs or contact details were hallucinated.

The source document consists of 3 pages containing 9 sequential instructions for connecting to Wi-Fi. All 9 instructions and their accompanying images are present in the output JSON in the correct order.
The source document does not contain any tables; it is an instructional guide with text and screenshots. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I have cross-referenced the text in the JSON elements against the OCR and visual content of the source PDF. No sentences or phrases were omitted from any of the instructional steps.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output JSON faithfully preserves the wording of the source. Punctuation like smart quotes (e.g., “Connect”) and apostrophes (e.g., SSID’s) is consistently maintained. No meaning-altering changes were observed.

checks.a_analysis: No content duplication was found in the converted JSON. Each step and corresponding image from the source PDF appears exactly once in the output, following the sequence of the original document.
The document title is correctly identified as a section title. There are no further subheadings in the source, so no hierarchy exists to be broken. The structure is flat and logical.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table structure corruption could occur.

The reading order in the JSON matches the sequential, step-by-step nature of the source document perfectly. The flow from text instruction to supporting screenshot is maintained throughout all three pages.

The source document contains a series of instructional steps presented as plain paragraphs rather than a formatted list. The JSON correctly represents these as paragraph elements. No semantic list formatting was expected.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The HTML contains 1 heading (the section title) while the JSON metadata lists 0 heading elements within its sections. This mismatch of 1 is well below the threshold of 3+ and is an acceptable normalization for accessibility where a section title is rendered as an H1.
There are no links present in the source document, and none were injected into the JSON output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML output faithfully reflects the structure of the source PDF. The title, instructional text, and placement of images follow the same linear sequence as the original document. Although the HTML is presented as a single continuous scrollable page versus the multi-page PDF format, the layout logic—instruction followed by a visual aid—is maintained correctly.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; therefore, there are no tables to evaluate in the HTML output.

The source PDF includes nine critical screenshots that visually guide the user through the login process. In the HTML output, only the first two images are rendered correctly. The remaining seven images (the Wi-Fi list, Connect button dialog, password entry field, connection progress, portal splash page, success confirmation, and final connection list) are missing and replaced by solid green rectangles. These missing images are essential for the document's utility as a guide.
checks.b_evidence_source: Nine screenshots across pages 1, 2, and 3 depicting the software UI (e.g., the Wi-Fi list on page 1, the security key entry on page 2, and the success screen on page 3).
checks.b_evidence_output: Seven solid green rectangles representing missing images for the Wi-Fi list, Connect button, Password prompt, Connection progress, Splash page, Success message, and final Connected status.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML output, appearing after the 'Select NCGov-Guest' text and continuing throughout the rest of the document.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of visual duplication in the HTML output. Each text instruction and image placeholder appears only once, corresponding to its unique occurrence in the source PDF.

Excluding the specific content failure of the broken images (captured in V3), the overall visual quality of the HTML conversion is good. The text is clear and readable, spacing between sections is appropriate, and the document structure is logical. The presence of the green blocks is a rendering issue for specific elements rather than a general degradation of formatting polish.
